name: Anthony Firkser
headline: Veteran depth flier with no fantasy footprint in Washington
body:
Firkser's 2025 tape leaves nothing to project upward. Across five games he logged only 116 routes for an 18.7% route share, drew 10 targets, and finished with eight catches for 53 yards and zero touchdowns. A 1.8% target share and 0.034 WOPR confirm he was barely a part of any passing plan, and 6.62 yards per reception with 5.12 air yards per target marks him as a short-area outlet, not a producer. The lone bright spot, three red-zone looks, never converted.

Now 31 and signed in Washington roughly two weeks ago, he projects to battle for the back end of the tight end room rather than meaningful snaps. Drafters echo that, slotting him around TE177 with a single expert even bothering to rank him and zero ADP. With no 2026 projection and roughly 0% rostered, he's a camp body and emergency depth, not a fantasy asset on any standard board.
